element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Fundamental fighting mechanics taught through opening tournament bracket
Each character's unique attack signature (medicine ball, morphing, headspin, etc.) revealed gradually as opponent variety increases
Distance management introduced via characters like Googoo (bottle throw) and Butch (fart attack)
Mirror matches with identical sprites but radically different movesets, stances, and attack patterns force player to unlearn and re-adapt
Final opponent is the player character's evil counterpart with inverted fighting philosophy and movepool

typical_tension_triggers: Evil counterpart introduction with different stance/coloring
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Tournament bracket structure provides escalating difficulty with natural breaks between matches; each stage features comedic character themes (jungle temple, toy store, boxing ring, military camp) that balance visual humor against combat intensity

spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Unlockable evil counterpart characters accessible through tournament completion or specific unlock conditions

difficulty_curve.early_game: Basic opponent patterns with single-specialty movesets (Nana Man's kicks, Tiny's medicine ball); teaching core fighting mechanics through variety
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased special move frequency and projectile attacks; opponents begin mixing attack patterns; stage hazards or environment interactions likely present
difficulty_curve.late_game: Evil counterparts with identical visual appearance but completely reworked stances, attack sequences, and special moves; requires unlearning expectations built from fighting the normal versions
notable_spikes: First evil counterpart encounter (dramatic movepool inversion)
Final boss (player's own evil twin with inverted fighting philosophy)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each playable character has a distinct thematic arena matching their personality and fighting style: Bad Mr. Frosty (North Pole), Tiny (castle foyer), Blob (clay-covered city street), Nana Man (jungle temple), Octohead (ocean floor), Googoo (toy store street), Hoppy (military camp), Kangoo (boxing ring). Arenas serve as visual reward progression.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Nana Man's jungle temple - laid-back aesthetic mirrors character's carefree Jamaican personality

notable_level_patterns.description: Tournament structure includes unlockable evil counterparts for each character with identical sprites but fundamentally different fighting mechanics: different battle stances, attack types, color schemes, win poses, and special moves. Creates cognitive dissonance and forces complete strategy re-evaluation.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Ice (Frosty's evil twin) vs Bad Mr. Frosty - boxing stance replaces Frosty's usual stance; Ice steals and repurposes Frosty's Sneaky Punch with different mechanics

notable_level_patterns.description: Linear tournament structure progresses through bracket rounds with escalating opponent skill and complexity. Each victory unlocks next bracket tier. Final opponent is always the player's selected character's evil counterpart, creating thematic narrative closure.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Standard bracket flow: introduction fighters → mid-tier specialists → final boss (evil counterpart)
